about.title: Who we are
about.description: Vardhishnu is a non-profit organization working toward protecting and empowering children from vulnerable backgrounds, particularly those involved in or at risk of child labor. We believe every child deserves a safe space to learn, grow, and thrive.

items.name: Anandghar
items.description: Community learning centers for children from vulnerable backgrounds to connect, share and reflect.

items.name: Anandghar Fellowship
items.description: Two year teaching fellowship where fellows are placed in community learning center where they work closely with children, parents, community and the schools.

items.name: Community Collectives
items.description: One year unique support program for grass-root initiaitves with limited or no exposure of changing developmental landscape

impact.title: From safe spaces to thriving futures - our impact in action

metrics.label: Children in partner organisations' project areas

footer.about: Work with local community for better future of their children

Our Advisors.name: Sunil Chavan
Our Advisors.bio: Mr. Sunil Chavan, Fondly Known As Sunil Kaka, Holds An M.Sc. In Statistics And An MBA In Marketing. After Spending 17 Years In The IT Sector, He Founded His Own Company In 1996, Which He Successfully Exited In 2012 To Dedicate Himself To The Social Sector. He Currently Serves As Director At Dr. M. L. Dhawale Trust, Palghar, And Is Also A Director At Bharat Rural Livelihood Foundation (BRLF). In Addition, He Is A Visiting Faculty Member At Shiv Nadar University, Where He Shares His Rich Experience With Young Leaders. Mr. Chavan Has Been Associated With Vardhishnu As A Member Of The Advisory Board Since 2016. A Seasoned Entrepreneur With Deep Expertise In Finance And Economics, He Provides Vardhishnu With Invaluable Strategic Guidance And Financial Counsel, Helping Strengthen The Organization’s Vision And Long-Term Sustainability.

whychildren.title: Why Children
whychildren.introduction: According to the United Nations Convention on the Rights of the Child (UNCRC), which India ratified in 1992, every child is entitled to fundamental rights that must be respected and upheld by both governments and citizens.

rights.title: Right to Survival
rights.content: which ensures a child’s right to be born, and to access food, shelter, clothing, healthcare, and live a life with dignity;

rights.title: Right to Protection
rights.content: which safeguards children from all forms of violence, neglect, abuse—both physical and sexual—as well as protection from harmful substances like drugs;

rights.title: Right to Participation
rights.content: which recognizes children’s freedom to express their views, access information, form associations, and take part in decisions that affect them directly;

rights.title: Right to Development
rights.content: which guarantees access to education, opportunities to learn, relax, play, and develop emotionally, mentally, and physically.
